title: False Positive: Root Login That Still Alerts
learning_objectives: Separate alerting from actual incident severity
Explain why a true alert can still be a false positive in context
Practice documenting a non-malicious reason for suspicious activity
triage_questions: What host and user were involved?
What evidence points to approved maintenance instead of compromise?
evidence_to_check: The root login event and the follow-up sudo activity
The source IP and host name involved in both events
The case story and message fields for maintenance context
hints: The alert is real. The question is whether the activity is malicious.
The same source IP and host show up across the short sequence.
Read the event messages closely before treating this as an incident.
escalation_guidance: Document it as a false positive alert tied to approved maintenance unless outside context contradicts the case evidence.
